semanticCore: Progeny, descendants, sliding down, rushing, walking fast, or exit
quranicRelevance: Human progeny/offspring (Nasl - 'thumma ja'ala naslahu'), and rushing down from graves (Yansiloon - 'yansiloon').

keyDerivations.meaning: Progeny, offspring, descendants, lineage, or children
To slide down, descend rapidly, run fast, or stream out

figurativeOrRareUsages: حَتَّىٰ إِذَا فُتِحَتْ يَأْجُوجُ وَمَأْجُوجُ وَهُمْ مِنْ كُلِّ حَدَبٍ يَنْسُلُونَ (21:96): Until when Gog and Magog are let loose, and they are sliding down/rushing from every elevated path.
summaryEn: The root connects progeny with rapid movement, describing the biological descent of human lineage (Nasl) and the rapid streaming (Yansiloon) of resurrected souls.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر بين النسل والذرية، والانسلال السريع والانحدار، واصفاً خلق سلالة النسل البشري، وانحدار يأجوج ومأجوج من التلال سريعاً.
rawText: اِنْتَسَلَ 8 said of camels' fur: see عَصِيمٌ. نَسْلٌ Progeny, whether of man or beast. (The Lexicons passim.) نَسْلًَا وَأَسْلًا is a form of imprecation against a man, like تَعْسًا وَنَكْسًا. (M, in art. اسل.)
